description:
Goal: define the semantic-to-artifact handoff so materialization consumes `SemanticClosed(root)` mechanically.

Change:
- Add `MaterializedProgram(root, semantic_revision)` or equivalent artifact fact keyed by the closed semantic root revision.
- Materialization reads only closed semantic facts plus root-independent lowered bodies named by those facts.
- It may fold, inline safe known calls, fuse, and prune inside the closed set, but it must not publish semantic facts or discover new reachability.
- Missing callee/type/return/callable/effect facts at materialization time are fatal incomplete-semantic-plan errors with details emitted through telemetry.

Why:
- Backend preparation must be a projection of settled semantic facts. If artifact work can ask semantic questions, `SemanticClosed` is not a real boundary.

Checks:
- Quicksort plus uncalled `foo/0` materializes no `foo/0` body.
- Enum.reduce materializes only the selected runtime/protocol/callable path.
- Tests prove artifact jobs emit no selected-callee, activation, executable, return-type, or planner-style facts.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: materialize only from SemanticClosed
